steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $11x+3y$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group the $x$ terms together: $7x + 4x = 11x$. The $y$ term has no like terms, so it stays as $3y$. Result: $11x + 3y$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $11x + 3y$. That matches Choice C — $11x+3y$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $14x$: Adds all three terms as if they were like terms and drops $3y$ entirely — $x$ and $y$ terms cannot be combined. ✗
  • Choice B — $14y$: Treats all terms as $y$ terms and uses the wrong sum — no basis for this. ✗
  • Choice C — $11x+3y$: $7x + 4x = 11x$ and $3y$ remains unchanged. ✓
  • Choice D — $28x+3y$: Multiplies the $x$ coefficients (7 × 4 = 28) instead of adding them. ✗
That matches Choice C — $11x+3y$.
